behaviors.description: Reject webhook when `X-Signature-256` does not match raw body; do not forward event.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: a unit test of just the HMAC function can miss wiring bugs (raw-body extraction, header parsing, middleware ordering). REAL-INFRA: no external network/process required. MOCK-HIDE: mocking request parsing could hide real mismatch handling, so at least module-wiring integration is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Start the app with real webhook middleware and a real in-process queue adapter (or test double at module boundary). Send an HTTP request with a deliberately bad signature over the app interface. Assert `401` response and assert queue write API was not called / queue storage remains unchanged.

behaviors.description: Persist event to queue before returning HTTP 200.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: with an in-memory mock queue, ordering bugs around persistence commit vs response can be missed. REAL-INFRA: needs real queue persistence path (DB/filesystem), but not external services. MOCK-HIDE: mocked enqueue that resolves immediately can hide race/ordering defects.
behaviors.test_description: Run handler with the real persistent queue implementation (local DB/filesystem). Instrument enqueue commit point and response send point. Send a valid webhook and assert commit timestamp/order occurs before `200` is sent.

behaviors.description: Return HTTP response without waiting for downstream event processing completion.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: unit tests of handler logic can miss real async wiring where processing is accidentally awaited. REAL-INFRA: no external network/process needed. MOCK-HIDE: if downstream is mocked as instant success, accidental waiting is hidden.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler + queue + processor module in-process. Configure processor to block (e.g., promise resolved after 10s). Send a valid webhook and assert HTTP response is returned quickly while processor is still running, then confirm processor completion later.

behaviors.description: Respond within 3 seconds even when earlier events are slow and concurrent.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: in-process mocks often miss event-loop contention, socket scheduling, and real concurrency effects. REAL-INFRA: this is wall-clock SLO under concurrent load, requiring real HTTP server/runtime behavior. MOCK-HIDE: mocked timing/concurrency can falsely pass.
behaviors.test_description: Launch the service in a real process. Create concurrent traffic with earlier events triggering intentionally slow processing. Send new valid webhooks over real HTTP and measure end-to-end latency; assert responses remain under 3s at the configured concurrency/load profile.

behaviors.description: Do not enqueue duplicate event IDs.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: dedupe usually depends on persistent uniqueness/lookup logic across modules, not just one function. REAL-INFRA: local DB/filesystem-backed queue is enough. MOCK-HIDE: mocked repository can miss unique-constraint/idempotency defects.
behaviors.test_description: Use real persistent queue storage with dedupe mechanism enabled. Send the same event ID twice (sequentially, and optionally concurrently). Assert first request enqueues once and second does not create a second stored queue record.

behaviors.description: When queue is full, return 503 and include `Retry-After`.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: mapping queue-full error to HTTP status/header can fail at module boundaries. REAL-INFRA: requires real queue behavior at capacity, but no external API/process necessarily. MOCK-HIDE: forcing a mocked error may miss real full-condition detection path.
behaviors.test_description: Configure real persistent queue with small capacity and fill it. Send another valid webhook. Assert response is `503`, `Retry-After` header is present and well-formed, and no new queue record is persisted.

behaviors.description: Persistent queue enforces configured capacity and rejects overflow.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: capacity enforcement often relies on storage-level behavior (transactions/counting/locks) beyond a single function. REAL-INFRA: local persistent store interaction is the key boundary. MOCK-HIDE: mocked queue internals can always accept writes and hide true overflow bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate the real queue against its persistent backend with capacity N. Attempt N+1 writes under realistic conditions (including a concurrent write case). Assert exactly N are accepted and overflow write(s) return the queue’s rejection error.
